title: The 14-club rule and what's in a bag
microFact: You may carry a maximum of 14 clubs. Carrying more costs penalty strokes in competition.
lesson:
The Rules allow at most 14 CLUBS in your bag. A typical composed set:

• DRIVER (1-wood): longest club, tee shots on long holes
• FAIRWAY WOOD (3W/5W) and/or HYBRIDS: long shots from the ground
• IRONS 5–9: the scoring workhorses; bigger number = shorter, higher shot
• WEDGES: pitching (PW), sand (SW ~56°), maybe gap/lob — short precision shots
• PUTTER: on the green

At your stage a driver, two hybrids, 6–9 irons, PW, 56° and putter (10–11 clubs) is plenty — fewer decisions, more confidence, and completely normal-looking at any club.

In competition, extra clubs cost two strokes per hole played with them in the bag (capped at four strokes). Count your clubs on the first tee — pros have lost tournaments to this (Ian Woosnam, 2001 Open, famously).

quiz.question: What's the maximum number of clubs allowed in your bag?
quiz.explanation: 14 is the limit. Playing with more brings penalties — count on the first tee.
